title: [cross-file] link_ctx_initialized vs lora_l2 ABORTED state desync
description:
Interaction: lichen_l2.c:link_ctx_initialized <-> lora_l2.c:current_state

Bug: When lichen_lora_l2_stop() returns -ECANCELED (RX thread aborted), the lora_l2 module enters ABORTED state. However, lichen_l2.c's link_ctx_initialized atomic remains set to 1. The lichen_l2_enable() code (line 979) checks lichen_lora_l2_needs_reinit() BEFORE checking link_ctx_initialized.

The code at line 1006 re-initializes link_ctx only if !atomic_get(&link_ctx_initialized). But after an abort, link_ctx_initialized is still 1 (never cleared), so the re-init is skipped. This means lichen_l2_enable() will call lichen_lora_l2_start() on a potentially corrupted link_ctx.

The only path that clears link_ctx_initialized on abort is the disable path (line 1146), but if the abort happened during enable(), the caller may not call disable() before retry.

Trigger:
1. lichen_l2_enable(true) called
2. RX thread started
3. RX thread hangs in lora_recv()
4. lichen_lora_l2_stop() times out and aborts thread -> returns -ECANCELED
5. Caller sees error, waits, then calls lichen_l2_enable(true) again
6. needs_reinit() returns true -> fails at line 980
7. Caller calls deinit() which clears state
8. Caller calls init() then enable(true)
9. link_ctx_initialized is still 1 from step 2
10. Re-init of link_ctx is skipped
close_reason: Fixed: Clear link_ctx_initialized when stop() returns -ECANCELED and deinit() fails, or when rx_mutex lock times out. This prevents skipped re-initialization on restart after abort. Two atomic_set calls added with security comments.
